name: Sierra Cruces – circular from Navamorcuende
region_intro: Hiking around Navamorcuende offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de San Vicente, a mountainous range characterized by scenic beauty and ecological richness. The region features prominent peaks like Cruces and San Vicente, providing panoramic views. Water bodies such as the Guadyerbas River and La Portiña Reservoir add to the natural environment, alongside varied vegetation including holm oaks and ash groves. Best hiking trails around Navamorcuende The most popular hiking route is Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26), a 4.5 miles (7.2 km) trail that takes 1 hour 56 minutes to complete. This easy route is suitable for all fitness levels. Another top favourite among local hikers is Senda de Viriato Loop from Almendral de la Cañada, a difficult 13.4 miles (21.5 km) path. This challenging route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Sierra Cruces – circular from Navamorcuende, a 6.7 miles (10.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 46 minutes. Hiking around Navamorcuende is defined by the Sierra de San Vicente mountain range, prominent peaks offering panoramic views, and diverse vegetation along river courses. The network of 61 routes offers options for different ability levels, including 18 easy, 39 moderate, and 4 difficult trails. The routes in Navamorcuende are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 400 hikers have used komoot to explore Navamorcuende's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Navamorcuende?
A: Navamorcuende offers a network of 61 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels. This includes 18 easy, 39 moderate, and 4 difficult trails, ensuring options for every hiker.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Navamorcuende?
A: Yes, for experienced trekkers, the region offers several challenging routes. One notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018253970/senda-de-viriato-loop-from-almendral-de-la-canada" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda de Viriato Loop from Almendral de la Cañada&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 13.4-mile (21.5 km) path featuring significant elevation gain and panoramic views.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The trails around Navamorcuende showcase the diverse landscapes of the Sierra de San Vicente. You'll find varied vegetation, including ash, willow, and alder groves along water courses, as well as holm oaks and junipers. The region is also home to the Guadyerbas River and the tranquil La Portiña Reservoir.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Navamorcuende are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. An easy circular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018792785/route-of-the-caleros-circular-from-montesclaros-pr-to-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.5 miles (7.2 km) long. For a moderate challenge, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1020993689/sierra-cruces-circular-from-navamorcuende" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Cruces – circular from Navamorcuende&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints accessible via hiking trails?
A: Hikers can enjoy impressive panoramic views from peaks like Cruces and Cerro San Vicente, offering glimpses of the Sierra de Gredos and the Alto Tiétar region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2607538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Piélago Pass&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent views of the Toledo steppe. You can also reach &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214626" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente Hill (1320m)&lt;/a&gt; on a loop from Campamento El Piélago.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Navamorcuende?
A: Yes, there are 18 easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018792785/route-of-the-caleros-circular-from-montesclaros-pr-to-26" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Caleros – circular from Montesclaros (PR-TO 26)&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice, being an easy 4.5-mile (7.2 km) trail suitable for all fitness levels.

Q: Can I find any trails that lead to water features or natural pools?
A: The region is rich in water bodies. The gorges of Gavilanes and Mijares feature natural pools and waterfalls, including the highest waterfall in the community in the Chorro gorge near Gavilanes. The La Portiña Reservoir also offers a tranquil setting with trails and opportunities for fishing.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Navamorcuende?
A: The routes in Navamorcuende are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the panoramic views from the peaks, and the variety of trails available for different abilities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navamorcuende?
A: Navamorcuende benefits from a benign climate without extreme temperatures in winter or summer, making it appealing for year-round outdoor exploration. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable conditions for hiking, with pleasant temperatures and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there any moderate hikes that offer good views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466527" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Cruces – Puerto del Piélago loop from Campamento El Piélago&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.5 km hike that provides good fitness challenges and scenic vistas. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31466558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto del Piélago – San Vicente Hill (1320m) loop from Campamento El Piélago&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Navamorcuende?
A: While specific regulations for each trail may vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in natural areas like the Sierra de San Vicente, especially if kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or park rules for any specific restrictions before heading out with your pet.
tips: The summit of Cruces at 1,373 meters above sea level is one of the most impressive panoramic points in the Navamorcuende region. It offers beautiful views in all directions so I recommend taking some time and enjoying the beautiful views while having a picnic up here to recharge your batteries.

Declared a Site of Cultural Interest, the church in the village of Navamorcuende is a building well worth a visit. Its Renaissance style stands out for its large buttresses, built in the 16th century, and the stone used takes on a distinctive hue on rainy days and in autumn.
